title: Bar Chart: Money given to developing countries from five organisations (2008–2011)
description: The chart below shows the amount of money given to developing countries from five organizations from 2008 to 2011. Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
guide_html:
HƯỚNG DẪN VIẾT BÀI

Investment by Organisations (2008–2011)
1. Hiểu đề bài

Biểu đồ cho thấy số tiền đầu tư (billion dollars) của 5 tổ chức qua 4 năm: 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011 → so sánh giữa các nhóm + xu hướng theo thời gian.

2. Cách viết Overview
  • Organisation 2 luôn đầu tư nhiều nhất và tăng mạnh
  • Organisation 4 luôn thấp nhất
  • Organisation 5 giảm rõ rệt theo thời gian
3. Cách chia Body
BODY 1 – Organisation 2 (tăng mạnh nhất)
  • 1.3 → 1.5 → 2.3 → 2.6 tỷ USD → tăng liên tục và mạnh nhất
BODY 2 – Các tổ chức còn lại
  • Organisation 1: 0.6 → 0.9 → 0.7 → 0.7 (tăng rồi ổn định)
  • Organisation 3: 1.5 → 1.8 → 1.4 → 1.5 (dao động)
  • Organisation 4: 0.7 → 0.4 → 0.5 → 0.4 (luôn thấp nhất)
  • Organisation 5: 1.2 → 0.8 → 0.5 → 0.5 (giảm rõ rệt)
4. Gợi ý từ vựng
  • increased significantly / fluctuated / declined steadily / remained the lowest / the highest overall value

The bar chart compares the amount of money (in billions of dollars) invested by five organisations between 2008 and 2011.

Overall, Organisation 2 consistently invested the most and showed a strong upward trend. In contrast, Organisation 4 recorded the lowest figures throughout, and Organisation 5 experienced a notable decline over the period.

In 2008, Organisation 3 was the largest investor at 1.5 billion dollars, followed by Organisation 2 at 1.3 billion. Organisation 5 contributed 1.2 billion, while Organisation 4 and Organisation 1 invested 0.7 billion and 0.6 billion respectively.

By 2009, Organisation 2 rose to 1.5 billion and Organisation 3 peaked at 1.8 billion. Organisation 1 increased to 0.9 billion, whereas Organisations 4 and 5 declined. In 2010 and 2011, Organisation 2 grew significantly to 2.3 and then 2.6 billion — the highest overall. Organisation 3 fell slightly to 1.4–1.5 billion, while Organisations 4 and 5 remained low at 0.4–0.5 billion.
